MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
Off Calendar Mar-12-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS JERIES AZAR AND 1429 GRANT AVE, LLC'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS.

Appearances: Andrew Linton (415.916.8994) plaintiff and responding party appearing in propria persona; Justin Goodman of Zacks & Freedman (415.956.8100) for defendant and moving party.

Argued and the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: GRANTED. All remaining claims are barred by issue preclusion, claim preclusion, failure to assert a cross-complaint, and be release in the settlement agreement. Order and judgment are signed. Judge: Harry L. Jacobs, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Karen Aufdermaur, CSR 10919 (T: 209.234.9532, E: kaufdermaur@yahoo.com) (501/HLJ)

Motion For Defendants Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Oct-14-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Plaintiff ANDREW LINTON, in pro per present (tel: 415-916-8994).
Atty. JUSTIN GOODMAN, Esq. of Zacks & Freedman, PC (tel: 415-956-8100) for Defendants.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT JERIES AZAR, AND 1429 GRANT AVE, LLC's Motion For Defendants Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings.

The Court holds a discussion regarding the two pending appeals in this matter. Upon conclusion of discussion, the Court exercises its inherent power and stays the matter pending a resolution on the two appeals.

Counsel Justin Goodman is to prepare a proposed order and email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Court reporter: Jane Hong, CSR# 11975 (tel: 909-831-2644 / email: csrjane@hotmail.com)

Motion For Defendants Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Sep-26-2024 Continued To Oct-14-24 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: Appearances

Andrew Linton, plaintiff in pro per via zoom
Justin Goodman Esq., Zacks & Freedman PC, 415-956-8100 via zoom for defendants

Mr. Linton and Mr. Goodman contest to the tentative. Due to scheduling conflicts, Mr. Goodman requests for a new hearing date. Matter discussed and parties agreed on a new hearing of Oct-14-24.

The Court rules as follows:
